description_long: This object is a concrete jersey barrier, commonly used for traffic control and safety in urban environments. The surface is predominantly gray with visible weathering, scratches, and patches of discoloration, indicating prolonged outdoor exposure. Along the lower edge, there are yellow and black diagonal hazard stripes, which serve as a visual warning for vehicles and pedestrians. One side of the barrier features two large, rough patches where the concrete appears chipped or eroded. The top and sides are flat and angular, with a trapezoidal cross-section that provides stability. The overall appearance suggests a robust, utilitarian structure designed to withstand impacts and environmental wear.
description: A weathered gray concrete jersey barrier with yellow-black hazard stripes and visible surface damage. It features a trapezoidal shape and rough, chipped areas on one side.
description_view_0: The front face shows a mostly intact gray surface with yellow-black hazard stripes along the bottom edge and minor surface wear.
description_view_1: A narrow end view reveals the trapezoidal profile and the thickness of the barrier, with visible greenish discoloration near the base.
description_view_2: The rear face displays two large, rough patches of chipped concrete, along with the same yellow-black hazard stripes at the bottom.
description_view_3: The opposite end view mirrors view 1, showing the thickness and angular profile with some greenish weathering.
